profiles.name: Monday - Cortisol Peak
profiles.training_emphasis: Moderate compound movements, RPE 6-7. Cortisol naturally peaks Monday morning - leverage for strength work but avoid excessive volume that compounds stress.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Anti-inflammatory emphasis. Prioritize omega-3s, colorful vegetables, avoid processed foods. Support immune system recovering from weekend.
profiles.intervention_focus: Cold shower (2-3 min) to reset inflammation. Meditation to manage cortisol. Red light therapy for mitochondrial support.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Cortisol peaks early Monday. Testosterone also elevated. Good window for challenging work but monitor recovery.
profiles.immune_notes: Immune function often lower after weekend social exposure. Prioritize zinc, vitamin C, elderberry if needed. Extra sleep if possible.

profiles.name: Tuesday - Anabolic Window
profiles.training_emphasis: High intensity day, RPE 8-9. Testosterone-cortisol ratio favorable. Push compound lifts: squat, deadlift, bench. Volume can be higher.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Higher carbohydrates around training (150-200g). Protein bolus post-workout (40-50g). Creatine timing optimal.
profiles.intervention_focus: Sauna post-workout (15-20 min) for growth hormone release. Avoid cold immediately post-training to preserve hypertrophy signal.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Anabolic hormones peaking mid-week. Best day for maximal effort. Growth hormone responsive to training stimulus.
profiles.immune_notes: Immune system generally stable. Brief immunosuppression post-intense training is normal - support with glutamine.

profiles.name: Wednesday - Anabolic Continuation
profiles.training_emphasis: Upper body focus or accessory work. RPE 7-8. Continue building on Tuesday's stimulus. Good day for hypertrophy-focused training.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Protein emphasis (1g/lb target). Moderate carbs. Include leucine-rich foods: eggs, whey, chicken breast.
profiles.intervention_focus: Contrast therapy if recovered (hot/cold cycles). Mobility work. Sleep optimization critical - aim for 8+ hours.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Anabolic environment continues. IGF-1 levels responsive. Good recovery day hormonal environment.
profiles.immune_notes: Stable immune function. Good day for vitamin D optimization if levels suboptimal. Sun exposure if available.

profiles.name: Thursday - Metabolic Flexibility
profiles.training_emphasis: Zone 2 cardio emphasis (60-75% max HR). Fasted training beneficial. 45-60 min steady state or longer easy effort.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Lower carbohydrate window (50-100g). Higher fat intake. Consider extended fast (16-20h) ending post-training. MCT oil, avocado, olive oil.
profiles.intervention_focus: Cold exposure more beneficial today - enhances fat oxidation. Brown fat activation. Morning cold shower or cold plunge.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Cortisol lowering mid-week. Good day for metabolic stress without high cortisol burden. Insulin sensitivity training.
profiles.immune_notes: Fasting activates autophagy - immune system cleanup. Don't fast if fighting infection. Bone broth to break fast.

profiles.name: Friday - Hormetic Stress
profiles.training_emphasis: Hard finish to training week. HIIT, heavy lifts, or challenging workout. RPE 8-9. Push limits before recovery weekend.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Refeed opportunity - higher carbs post-workout (200-300g). Replenish glycogen. Reward meal acceptable. Still prioritize protein.
profiles.intervention_focus: Full protocol day: training + sauna (20 min) + cold plunge (3-5 min). Maximum hormetic stress before weekend recovery.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Testosterone may be lower end of week but still responsive to stimulus. Growth hormone spike from combined stressors.
profiles.immune_notes: Immune system will be challenged by combined stressors. Ensure zinc, vitamin C, sleep. Don't push if already run down.

profiles.name: Saturday - Social Recovery
profiles.training_emphasis: Active recovery only. Mobility work, yoga, easy walk, recreational activity. RPE 3-4 maximum. No structured training.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Flexible eating within reason. Social meals acceptable. Maintain protein intake. Hydration focus. Moderate alcohol if any.
profiles.intervention_focus: Prioritize social connection - oxytocin benefits. Massage or self-myofascial release. Nature exposure. Sunlight.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Recovery day allows hormone normalization. Avoid training that spikes cortisol. Let anabolic recovery processes complete.
profiles.immune_notes: Social connection supports immune function. Laughter, touch increase immune markers. Avoid overindulgence that impairs immune function.

profiles.name: Sunday - Melatonin Peak
profiles.training_emphasis: Rest or very light Zone 2 only (30-45 min easy). Prepare body for Monday. No intensity. Walking, gentle cycling acceptable.
profiles.nutrition_focus: Gut-friendly focus. Fermented foods: kimchi, sauerkraut, yogurt. Prebiotic fiber. Early dinner (finish eating by 6-7pm). 12-14h overnight fast.
profiles.intervention_focus: Red light therapy evening. Blue light blocking after sunset. Prepare sleep environment. Gratitude practice.
profiles.hormonal_notes: Melatonin production critical tonight. Dim lights early. Natural melatonin production supports immune function and recovery.
profiles.immune_notes: Gut-immune axis day. 70% of immune system in gut. Feed beneficial bacteria. Avoid gut irritants. Set up strong Monday.
